title: rubus lusitanicus
intro: Rubus lusitanicus, also known as the Portuguese blackberry or bramble, is a species of thorny shrub native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant's leaves, stems, and roots have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus lusitanicus include its ability to reduce oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
